issue_title: Implement negative log-likelihood as an optional error metric
issue_body:
@chetan51  @subutai 
All the existing error metrics consider only the single best prediction from the CLAModel. In scenarios where multiple predictions are possible, it is more appropriate to use the negative log-likelihood as the error metrics. Given the predicted probability of all possible outcomes, likelihood is the predicted probability of the actual outcome. The log-likelihood is an additive metric and can be accumulated like many of the existing error metrics. 

A recent example of using likelihood as the error metric can be found at
http://arxiv.org/abs/1412.3555  (section 4.1)

Proposed changes
https://www.evernote.com/l/AZ3TtrOnOIhCYIMeLApI104iWlahr3ZM8hI

There are cases where anomaly_likelihood._filterLikelihoods() does not behave as expected -- moves an anomaly into the yellowzone when it should not. This has been evident in the Taurus app and needs to be tested in anomaly_likelihood_test.py.

issue_title: Use descriptive names for serialization schemas
issue_body: I'm working on port serialization of nupic to capnproto and have some questions related to field names. At moment, I'd leave the names as are in order to agilize the review of my PRs. But my ittention is (once serialization job is done but not officially introduced) to create a PR only to uniformize and make more descriptive the fields of all schemas (and in the future of their respective class members) and avoid re-work. For example, currently SpatialPoolerProto uses synPermMax while TemporalMemoryV2Proto (TemporalMemory) uses permanenceMax and TemporalMemoryV1Proto (TP) uses permMax. So maybe it's better we use something like synapsePermanenceMax for all schemas. Furthermore, names like numNewSynapsesPerSegment sounds better than newSynapsesCount in addiction to the infamous "w" in ScalarEncoder. In the begining, this refactoring will be limited to schema fields but the idea is it extend to class members names. I think this should be done as soons as possible (i.e. while new serialization is not official)

issue_title: Update license from GPLv3 to AGPLv3
Account for chunk-extension values when parsing chunked responses
issue_body:
When ipwb replay parses the chunked responses, it does not consider the potential extensions values per https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc2616#section-3.6.1 and in discussion at https://github.com/oduwsdl/ipwb/pull/124#discussion_r103848851. Check if a semi-colon is present and if so, parse out the chunk size as the first value rather than relying on the entirety of the line.

Via @ibnesayeed

issue_title: Watchmaker not setting the correct salt grain for Windows role (DC vs MS)
issue_body:
When attempting to run watchmaker with the extra parameter for a Windows Domain Controller; `watchmaker --log-level debug --log-dir=C:\Watchmaker\Logs --ash-role DomainController`
Watchmaker does not appear to be setting the correct grain for ash-windows to apply the DomainController settings.

The ash-windows formula appears to be looking for the grain `ash-windows:lookup:role`, but watchmaker is setting the grain `ash-windows:role`

https://github.com/plus3it/ash-windows-formula/blob/master/ash-windows/map.jinja#L53-L56

https://github.com/plus3it/watchmaker/blob/develop/src/watchmaker/workers/salt.py#L729-L731

After promotion of the DC and execution of the above watchmaker run, salt_call.debug.log shows multiple references to the _MS files rather than the _DC files that should be applied to a Domain Controller.

I am getting an exception when I use `GoogleNews-vectors-negative300.bin` with torchtext v0.2.3 and Python 3.6.

```python
vectors = Vectors(name='GoogleNews-vectors-negative300.bin', cache='/directory/to/word2vec')
```

The exception is `ValueError: could not convert string to float`. For each line of the utf-8 binary word2vec file, torchtext currently splits it into word and the word vector like this:

```python
entries = line.rstrip().split(b" " if binary_lines else " ")
word, entries = entries[0], entries[1:]
```

However, my `entries` after this block of code executes has a length of 3 for the first non-header line in GoogleNews-vectors-negative300.bin, which corresponds to `</s>`.

I propose we first decode each line and then split by " ". What do you think? Thanks!
I bet you can quantize the vector elements to 8 bit and make the index 4x smaller – this should help with cache locality as well as fitting more trees in the same index. Will try at some point

Hey,

I'm working on using z3 for a symbolic execution engine. Internally, I store the variables in a class dict structure. I noticed in my program that the app seems to be working as expected, however when I exit python, it always crashes with the following error:

```bash
*** Error in `/home/user/.virtualenvs/z3/bin/python3': corrupted double-linked list: 0x0000000001d9c940 ***
Aborted (core dumped)
```

After a little digging, I discovered the following if/set line appears to be reliably causing the problem:

```python
        if varName not in self.localVars:
            self.localVars[varName] = {
                'var': z3.Int(varName),
                'expr': []
            }
```

This is inside the python class I mentioned previously. varName == a string (say 'x'), self.localVars is a structure for the class as follows:

```python
    localVars = {
        'x': {
            'var': x, # This is the actual z3.Int/z3.Array, etc object
            'expr': [
                'localVars['x']['var'] > 1',    # Actual expressions to go into solver
                'localVars['x']['var'] * 5 > 7'
            ]
        }
    }
```

As I mentioned, the code works while running, but it appears that the exit procedure might be clearing up the links to the z3 variables before the variables are free'd themselves? In any case, not sure what to do about this.

issue_title: Not waiting for connection_lost() when transport closed
issue_body:
## Long story short

aiohttp calls `close()` on the transport and then immediately discards it without waiting for `connection_lost()`. This is a problem for SSL transports, which have to shut down asynchronously, and it appears aiohttp only "works" with the standard library's SSL transport because of a bug in that transport.

## Expected behaviour

aiohttp waiting for connection_lost() before discarding transport

## Actual behaviour

It not doing so

## Steps to reproduce

    import aiohttp
    import asyncio

    async def main():
        conn = aiohttp.TCPConnector(verify_ssl=False)
        async with aiohttp.ClientSession(connector=conn) as session:
            async with session.get('https://httpbin.org/') as resp:
                resp.raise_for_status()

    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    try:
        loop.run_until_complete(main())
    finally:
        loop.close()

Run this program and breakpoint `connection_made()` and `connection_lost()` in client_proto.py. The former is called, but not the latter. 

The program appears to run successfully, but to convince you that something is severely amiss, add a `__del__` method to the class `_SelectorSocketTransport` in asyncio's selector_events.py and breakpoint it and that transport's `close()` method. You'll see that `close()` is never called and `__del__` gets called during shutdown, after the loop has been closed and the module finalised, because it still has a reader registered and is therefore leaked (while still waiting on I/O) in a reference cycle with the event loop.

If I'm understanding it right, the overall behaviour appears to be due to two bugs, one in asyncio and one in aiohttp:

1. When `close()` is called on the SSLProtocolTransport in asyncio's ssl_proto.py it initiates an asynchronous shutdown process, which should ultimately result in it calling `connection_lost()`. However, this doesn't progress for reasons I haven't fully figured out, so `connection_lost()` is never called. The shutdown process is clearly broken because it never results in `close()` being called on the underlying transport, which is leaked as shown above.
2. aiohttp doesn't wait for `connection_lost()`, so the hang that one would expect as a result of this never happens. By luck, the broken shutdown sequence never actually causes any errors.

I'm actually more interested in the latter, the aiohttp behaviour, because I've written my own SSL transport (based on pyopenssl), which does a correct asynchronous shutdown sequence. When aiohttp discards my transport without waiting for `connection_lost()`, it causes errors, which I don't think are my fault.

## Your environment

Tested on Windows and Linux with python 3.5.1 and 3.5.2 and aiohttp 2.0.7 and 2.1.0.

issue_title: Websocket does not register disconnected Safari browser
issue_body:
With [Websockets](https://websockets.readthedocs.io), when Safari is reloaded or tab closed, the websocket server registers a closed connection. This is not the case with Aiohttp. But Aiohttp correctly registers the disconnection with Chrome. I don't know anything about the websocket protocol internals, but I guess there is a difference in what both browsers send on tab close/reload. In any case, Websockets somehow registers that event in both browsers, but Aiohttp doesn't.

Take the following app. When going to http://localhost:8080/websockets and reloading the site several times, the app emits alternating "Got client", "Lost client" with both Safari and Chrome. Now go to http://localhost:8080/aiohttp: only Chrome triggers lost connections.

```python
import asyncio
from aiohttp import web
import websockets

site = """\
<!doctype html>
<script>
const ws = new WebSocket(`ws://{href}`);
</script>
<body></body>"""


async def websockets_handler(ws, path):
    print('Got client')
    while True:
        try:
            print(await ws.recv())
        except websockets.ConnectionClosed as e:
            break
    print('Lost client')


async def aiohttp_ws_handler(request):
    ws = web.WebSocketResponse(autoclose=False)
    await ws.prepare(request)
    print('Got client')
    async for msg in ws:
        print(msg)
    print('Lost client')
    return ws


async def site_handler(request):
    if request.path == '/aiohttp':
        href = 'localhost:8080/ws'
    elif request.path == '/websockets':
        href = 'localhost:6060'
    return web.Response(text=site.format(href=href), content_type='text/html')


asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(websockets.serve(websockets_handler, 'localhost', 6060))

app = web.Application()
app.router.add_get('/aiohttp', site_handler)
app.router.add_get('/websockets', site_handler)
app.router.add_get('/ws', aiohttp_ws_handler)
web.run_app(app, host='localhost', port=8080)
```

issue_title: When installed with pip, gnureadline won't override system readline module
issue_body: When gnureadline is installed with pip, it will not override the system readline module. This means when dxpy is installed with pip, the injected gnureadline dependency doesn't do anything. The most durable solution is to try to import `gnureadline` instead of importing `readline` at the point of use, falling back to `readline` if the import fails.

issue_labels: [{'id': 631592722, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw2MzE1OTI3MjI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/Good%20First%20Issue', 'name': 'Good First Issue', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}, {'id': 815420353, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw4MTU0MjAzNTM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/comp:%20trinity', 'name': 'comp: trinity', 'color': 'bfd4f2', 'default': False}, {'id': 983629570, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjk1NzA=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/effort:%20E1%20hours', 'name': 'effort: E1 hours', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}, {'id': 983628230, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2MjgyMzA=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/prio:%20P3%20important', 'name': 'prio: P3 important', 'color': 'fad8c7', 'default': False}, {'id': 983615043, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2MTUwNDM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/type:%20performance', 'name': 'type: performance', 'color': 'eeeeee', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Some PeerPoolSubscribers (e.g. `LightPeerChain`, `TxPool`) are only meant to handle a subset of msg types, so it is rather wasteful to have `PeerPool` deliver all messages to their queues.

### How can it be fixed

We could make it possible for `PeerPoolSubscribers` to specify what types of msgs they aren't interested in, so that they never get those. Or they could specify the types of msgs they are interested in, but the problem with that is we need to remember to update the list if/when they're extended to handle more msg types

### What is wrong?
SMC utils (`SMC (SMCHandler)` and `ShardTracker`) are invoked in Trinity. And currently, there's no error handling in both sides.

#### Exception Types
1. API exceptions
    * Known general exceptions (e.g., `ValueError`)
    * Custom exceptions: 
        * handler: [sharding/exceptions.py at master · ethereum/sharding · GitHub](https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/master/sharding/handler/exceptions.py)
        * `BlockNotFound`: https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/717975aae7361bf738a51365c0f60cf97a594970/sharding/handler/log_handler.py#L9
2. `web3.py` exceptions [web3.py/exceptions.py at master · ethereum/web3.py · GitHub](https://github.com/ethereum/web3.py/blob/master/web3/exceptions.py)
    * If the provider fails to respond, `CannotHandleRequest` will be thrown and web3 will handle it and continue with next provider.
    * If all providers fail to respond, web3 will throw `UnhandledRequest`.
3. Other unexpected exceptions

### How can it be fixed

#### Custom Errors:
1. **[TODO]** Examine will sharding utils throw the right exceptions.
2. Known exceptions:
    * `LogParsingError`: it seems only happening when ABI versions don't match.
    * `BlockNotFound`: [it](https://github.com/ethereum/sharding/blob/717975aae7361bf738a51365c0f60cf97a594970/sharding/handler/log_handler.py#L38-L44) happens when the `from_block` parameter is larger than current block number. "How to set the suitable `from_block`" may depend on the syncing procedure. For now, with the current syncing procedure, the simple solution may be (1) adjusting `from_block` to `current_block_number - current_block_number % self.period_length` and then (2) retry.

#### `web3.py` exceptions:
1. **[TODO]** Examine which exceptions would be raise to `SMC` and how to deal them.
2. If it's retriable
    * For the transaction-calls:
        * Try to make sure whether the transaction is sent or not. (**Q: How to know it?**)
            * If sent, do nothing.
            * If not sent, retry. (**Q: Maximum retries?**)
    * For the polling tasks (e.g., querying `added_header` logs):
        * Retry (**Q: Maximum retries?**)

#### Other unexpected exceptions
Log the error and call `cleanup()`.

* Version: v4.0.0-beta5
*  Python: 3.5
*  OS: linux



### What was wrong?

```

print (signed)

issue_title: UserWarning when passing nested fields to AdAccount.get_ads()
issue_body:
Hi all,

For a reporting solution I need to download the entire account structure with select fields for each hierarchy level. While the Graph API allows me to make a nested request such as
`https://graph.facebook.com/v2.8/act_<account_id>/ads?fields=id,name,adlabels,adset{id,name,adlabels}&access_token=<access_token>`,
I have not found a way to replicate the same logic using the SDK.

When using the same nested field declaration as in the Graph API (`adset{id,name,adlabels}`) - e.g. in
`ad_account.get_ads(field=['id', 'name', 'campaign_id', 'adlabels'])`, 
it raises a `UserWarning: ads does not allow field adset{id,name,adlabels}`. 

Is there a different way to pass such a nested request to the `fields`-argument? If not, is there a particular reason for the warning while the Graph API is perfectly fine with this type of request or is this due to the automatic creation of the code?

Thanks for the support!

issue_title: memoization behaviour prevents memory to be released.
issue_body:
memoization in NeuroM involves a class variable: https://github.com/BlueBrain/NeuroM/blob/master/neurom/utils.py#L44
When accessing properties on a lot of morphologies sequentially, eventhough we don't reference the morphology anymore, properties are still stored in that cache.
The consequence is that memory keeps growing even if we actually needs 1 morphology at a time. This prevents to analyze large number of morphologies.
Please make that memoization optional or attach the cache to the morphology.

Add a distribution that simply returns the next value in a list/generator.

e.g.

```
'Service_distributions`: ['Sequential`, [0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 0.3]]

>>> Q.service_times[0][0]()
0.1

0.2

0.5
>>> Q.service_times[0][0]()
0.3

issue_title: echartsgl enabled 3D charts cannot use 'svg' renderer
issue_body:
![screen shot 2018-01-18 at 21 54 58](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4280312/35124292-795c522c-fc9d-11e7-81dd-917a3cb884df.png)
![screen shot 2018-01-18 at 21 55 33](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4280312/35124296-7bdc0006-fc9d-11e7-89dd-4ae10861a297.png)

Originally reported by: **Daniel Plachotich (Bitbucket: [danpla](http://bitbucket.org/danpla), GitHub: [danpla](http://github.com/danpla))**

----------------------------------------

A little performance test:

```
from timeit import timeit
import struct
from mutagen._util import cdata

N = b'\x00\x01\x02\x03'

def test_cdata():
    return cdata.uint_be(N)

def test_struct():
    return struct.unpack('>I', N)[0]

print(timeit(test_cdata))
print(timeit(test_struct))
```

```
python3 test.py
0.6052165959990816
0.33906224599923007

python2 test.py
0.507047891617
0.349157094955

```

`cdata` is very slow, limited, and inconvenient compared to `struct`. For example, imagine how much faster [this](https://bitbucket.org/lazka/mutagen/src/50a23d2f9c375f3599ff94e72e739466a9d91ff7/mutagen/_mp3util.py?at=default&fileviewer=file-view-default#_mp3util.py-374) will be using `struct` in single call.

In addition, it's misleading. Some modules even use both `cdata` and `struct`.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/lazka/mutagen/issue/248

issue_title: SSL verification disabled, without option to enable
issue_body:
On debian stretch I get warnings:
`/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/urllib3/connectionpool.py:852: InsecureRequestWarning: Unverified HTTPS request is being made. Adding certificate verification is strongly advised. See: https://urllib3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/advanced-usage.html#ssl-warnings
  InsecureRequestWarning)
`
I found at https://github.com/F5Networks/f5-icontrol-rest-python/blob/1.0/icontrol/authtoken.py#L98
the request is made with verify=False. It would be nice to be able to change this setting through F5 libraries to be able to verify ssl.
Anyway thank you for great work to make icontrol available from python.

`enum` is a part of standard library in Python 3.4, and there's an official backport for earlier Python versions (enum34 on pypi). It would probably make sense to ensure it can be serialized out of the box...

I guess the problem is that jsonpickle tries to serialize type `Foo` which has a metaclass (`enum.EnumMeta`), so it tries to pickle `enum.IntEnum` which fails since `IntEnum` has `__getnewargs__` method, but it's bound and has to be called on an instance and not a type.

So, these kind of checks can easily go wrong:
```python
has_getnewargs = hasattr(obj, '__getnewargs__')  # in pickler.py
```
e.g.:
```python

issue_title: There is no check on the channel value of otLinkSetChannel
issue_body:
The value provided to `otLinkSetChannel` is directly passed to `Mac::SetChannel`. In this function `OT_ERROR_NONE` is always returned. Therefore, `otLinkSetChannel` will never generate an error when an invalid channel number is provided.

wpanctl in wpantund will default the channel to 0
`target_network.channel = strtol(optarg, NULL, 0);` (wpantund/src/wpanctl/tool-cmd-join.c:97)
which eventually gets passed to `otLinkSetChannel`

File.mapentries() always throws error because of undefined variable.

File.py line 144 is:
`            return (keymap,entries)`
but should be:
`            return entries`

variable keymap is not defined, so code throws misleading exception of type LookupError

issue_title: Unhandled Attribute error during `filedeps` with v2 engine.
issue_body:
$ ./pants --enable-v2-engine filedeps abc:: 
...
Exception message: 'Address' object has no attribute 'build_file'

In the v1 engine an Address is resolved to a BuildFileAddress, in the v2 engine thats not the case -- hence causes this exception in filedeps and other goals.

issue_title: ‘Unrecognized arguments’ error misleading when plugin not installed
issue_body:
When the requested plugin hasn't been found, please give the error message about that as a higher priority to the one about unrecognized arguments. The unrecognized arguments may well be because of the missing plugin, so that's the first issue that the user needs to address.

I first tried this command:

    $ ./letsencrypt-auto certonly --webroot -w /srv/example/www -d www.example.org

Which yields this error message:

    letsencrypt: error: unrecognized arguments: -w /srv/example/www

That's misleading, giving little clue as to the actual problem. However, if I try omitting the complained-of arguments:

    $ ./letsencrypt-auto certonly --webroot -d www.example.org

It gives the more relevant error message:

    The requested webroot plugin does not appear to be installed

That explains why `-w` wasn't recognized. It'd be much more helpful to give that error message in the first place, and not quibble over what to do about optional arguments when there's the much more significant issue of the entire plugin being missing.

issue_title: Update all examples to follow model standard file setup
issue_body:
We created stand file setups for our models. You can see these in [Schelling](https://github.com/projectmesa/mesa/tree/pyconsprints/examples/Schelling) and [WolfSheep](https://github.com/projectmesa/mesa/tree/pyconsprints/examples/WolfSheep). 

Schelling is an example of a very simple model setup, while WolfSheep is an example of a more complex setup. To update you will need to do the following...

- Update the notebook to analysis and to use the new file structure

issue_title: Speed up actor ID (and other random ID generation).
issue_body:
This function is really slow (on the order of 5-10ms). It is used in a bunch of places and should be replaced with something faster (e.g., a deterministic hash). This is an issue e.g., for actor creation.

https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/cae108d019a51bd7bd35df7f93b68ff07a84d9cd/python/ray/utils.py#L84-L107

I'm not sure if a feature like this exceeds the scope of this project,
but i personally needed an option to view the quicklooks of queried tiles.

I'm using something like this:

```
from PIL import Image
import io
def show_quicklook(id_,username,password):
      url = 
      "https://scihub.copernicus.eu/apihub/odata/v1/Products('{}')/Products('Quicklook')/$value".format(id_)
      bytes_img = requests.session().get(url, auth=(username,password)).content
      return Image.open(io.BytesIO(bytes_img))

```
Of course a caveat is that it's only useful if the query size is not huge.

issue_title: Re-subscribe to a topic after disconnection/reconnection
issue_body:
Here is my test scenario. It's limited to the bare basics to isolate my problem

I have single kafka broker. Single topic, with a single partition.

Producer creates random messages to topic patricktest
Consumer reads the messages from topic patricktest

Producer
```python
from confluent_kafka import Producer
from time import gmtime

p = Producer({'bootstrap.servers':'localhost'})
p.produce('patricktest','Kwak kwak I got data on %s' % gmtime())
p.flush()
```

Consumer
```python
from confluent_kafka import Consumer, KafkaError
import sys
import time

c = Consumer({
  'bootstrap.servers':'localhost',
  'group.id':'patricklaptop',
  'default.topic.config': {'auto.offset.reset':'smallest'}
})

c.subscribe(['patricktest'])


while True:

  msg = c.poll(timeout=1.0)

  if msg is None:
    print "msg is None"
    #time.sleep(1)
    continue


  if msg.error():
    if msg.error().code() == KafkaError._PARTITION_EOF:
      # Not an error really
      sys.stderr.write('%% %s [%d] reached end at offset %d\n' % (msg.topic(), msg.partition(), msg.offset()))
    else:
      print "raising exception"
      raise KafkaException(msg.error())
  else:
    # Good stuff here.
    print('Received message: %s' % msg.value().decode('utf-8'))

  print "end of while cycle"

c.close()
```


So as you can see, it's pretty basic. In my test scenario, I run the consumer, and it will read the messages, then print "msg is None" quite a bit, until I produce more messages by running the producer code.

Now here is the problem: if I stop kafka while the consumer is running, then restart kafka, the consumer will reconnect BUT it won't re-subscribe to the topic.
I have not found ANY WAY to know that I was disconnected. All I know is that poll returns None. That's it.

For now the workaround that I have found is to add this at the beginning of the loop.

```python
last_resubscribe = 0
while True:
  # resub every 10sec just in case I got disconnected
  if time.time() - last_resubscribe > 10:
    print "resubscribing - just in case"
    c.subscribe(['patricktest'])
    last_resubscribe = time.time()

[...]
```

But sadly I also get this kind of thing every 10 seconds on kafka server:

kafka/server.out
```
[2016-11-04 22:26:27,851] INFO [GroupCoordinator 1]: Preparing to restabilize group patricklaptop with old generation 7 (kafka.coordinator.GroupCoordinator)
[2016-11-04 22:26:27,852] INFO [GroupCoordinator 1]: Stabilized group patricklaptop generation 8 (kafka.coordinator.GroupCoordinator)
[2016-11-04 22:26:28,114] INFO [GroupCoordinator 1]: Assignment received from leader for group patricklaptop for generation 8 (kafka.coordinator.GroupCoordinator)
```

Is there any way to catch the reconnect so that I can re-subscribe to a topic?
Or maybe have a way to query the consumer object to know my current status?
Or maybe have the client automatically resubscribe to the topics itself?

While my workaround is functional, it's pretty awful AND produces extra load/logs on the broker.
I'm suspecting that it would even trigger a full rebalance and other things if I had several consumers / partitions rather than this minimal one consumer / partition / topic minimal scenario...

Thanks!
--Patrick

issue_title: Improve the 'dataset/resource' card in a territory page
Hessian filter implementation is very slow
issue_body: Computing the largest eigenvalue of the Hessian matrix (`hessian_matrix` in `skimage/feature/corner.py`) on a 1250 x 1250 image takes about 3s on my machine, but is virtually instantaneous in Fiji, which isn't doing anything smart... Not sure whether `ndi.convolve` is to blame, but we can probably try to separate the kernels to use convolve1d, or other tricks.

## Description
I tried to use felzenszwalb's segmentation algorithm in skimage, but some part of its doc is really confusing (at least for me).

Firstly, the `scale` parameter is described as `Free parameter. Higher means larger clusters.` but only after I read 
[the source code][1], I found it is exactly the constant `k` in the threshold function that described in the [original paper][2]. So I think the doc should add something like `the k used in threshold function`. By the way, I wonder why the default of `scale` is 1 but not a larger number that the author used like 150 or 300 which works better in common cases?

And secondly, I do not really understand that why `sigma` was described as `Width of Gaussian kernel used in preprocessing` especially the word `width`. But I think it is the `sigma` is standard deviation used in 2-D Gaussian function, isn't it?


[1]:https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/segmentation/_felzenszwalb_cy.pyx#L108
[2]:http://people.cs.uchicago.edu/~pff/papers/seg-ijcv.pdf

This is the reproducible demo [BasicExample.zip](https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3/files/2030925/BasicExample.zip)

This problem is similar to the issue #1623 where the returned penetration is positive.
I've noticed that by setting static body extension from `128, 1, 128` to `1,1,1` the problem disappear.
Also the problem doesn't show up if I remove the line with `kinShape->setMargin(0);`

I'm not familiar with merges etc sorry. Thought I would share maybe make it into dev branch

printer.py line 155

    def __init__(self, host, port=9100, timeout=60, *args, **kwargs):
        """

        :param host : Printer's hostname or IP address
        :param port : Port to write to
        """
        Escpos.__init__(self, *args, **kwargs)
        self.host = host
        self.port = port
        self.timeout = timeout
        self.open()

    def open(self):
        """ Open TCP socket with ``socket``-library and set it as escpos device """
        self.device = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
        self.device.settimeout(self.timeout)
        self.device.connect((self.host, self.port))

        if self.device is None:
            print("Could not open socket for {0}".format(self.host))

issue_body:
Hi,

I was using the this library and I was getting some quite hard to debug/reproduce behaviour. This took me and a nice guy from PyCharm a while to figure out.

The issue is that the library's imports seem to break depending on the file name. For example:

```python
# graphql.py
import graphene
graphene.ObjectType
```
```bash
$ python graphql.py

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "graphql.py", line 1, in <module>
    import graphene
  File "/usr/local/var/pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/__init__.py", line 19, in <module>
    from .types import (
  File "/usr/local/var/pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/__init__.py", line 7, in <module>
    from .scalars import Scalar, String, ID, Int, Float, Boolean
  File "/usr/local/var/pyenv/versions/3.6.1/lib/python3.6/site-packages/graphene/types/scalars.py", line 3, in <module>
    from graphql.language.ast import (BooleanValue, FloatValue, IntValue,
  File "/Users/jamesstidard/Desktop/graphql.py", line 2, in <module>
    graphene.ObjectType
AttributeError: module 'graphene' has no attribute 'ObjectType'
```

I've noticed this for files named both `graphql` and `graphene`. I assume it has to do with how graphene is handling its imports and `__init__.py`. I did try to have a look through it, but after already spending the time trying to debug it, and that the `__init__.py` in Graphene isn't your straight forward one, my brain is currently fried.

I can fix my project by simply renaming the model to something like `graph_ql.py`, though I thought it's worth reporting as a bug as other people may also have this problem. Also depending on what's causing this, there may be a chance even more generic things like `utils`.

Thanks for the library.

Not sure how much time I have the next few weeks but wanted to get the ball rolling on `v0.6.0+`. I think it is probably best to target the sub-relsease rather than straight to `0.7.0` making it easier chunks.

In any case, here is the list of commits: https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/compare/v0.6.0...v0.6.1

As soon as I find some time I'll get started on it.

Guys,

`JSONField` was added just in Django 1.9[1] and in graphene code try import that as version 1.8.*
This could cause problem to `convert_postgres_array_to_list`, because if I'm using django 1.8 graphene_django will try import JSONField causing a ImportError and broke all the others imports.

A possible solution below: 

```python
try:
    # Postgres fields are only available in Django 1.8+
    from django.contrib.postgres.fields import ArrayField, HStoreField, RangeField
except ImportError:
    ArrayField, HStoreField, RangeField = (MissingType, ) * 3


try:
    # Postgres fields are only available in Django 1.9+
    from django.contrib.postgres.fields import JSONField
except ImportError:
    JSONField = MissingType
```

[1] - https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.9/releases/1.9/
[2] - https://github.com/graphql-python/graphene-django/blob/master/graphene_django/compat.py#L23

issue_title: Settle handle missing and old balance proofs
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

> The state machine assumes the partner will always successfully call update transfer, and includes both balance proofs in the settlement call, if the update transaction fails then the settle transaction will also fail. To fix this the settle state change should not include the balance proof, but the balance hash, and the balance proof should be recovered from the WAL.

Additionally the state machine assumes always the latest balance proof is used, if an old balance proof is used the state changes in database needs to be searched to find the correct balance proof data

related to: #2129
Every node should log its transfers to a journal.

issue_title: Fix setup.py when using plain distutils
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/629086, @zseil reported:

> Support for installing zope.interface with plain distutils has deteriorated a bit over the recent releases. There are currently two problems with the setup script - the list of packages is incomplete (reported as bug #611362) and the coptimization extension never gets built when not using setuptools. The attached patch fixes both problems for me. It also removes Python 2.3 support from the setup script, since zope.interface itself doesn't support that version anymore.
>
> (Tested on Windows, Python 2.5 with a C compiler and Python 2.6 without a C compiler, in both cases without setuptools installed)

with patch:  https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/629086/+attachment/1537986/+files/distutils-compatibility.patch

This is probably not a bug of GS, but tbh I am not sure where this bug belongs, I'll add it to plone.app.testing as well :-)

Some context:
When using the PloneSandbox layer in tests, the layer will create a demostorage and wrap the current storage. it will also "reset" the zcml config, doesn't matter, as the layer will rerun the zcml config anyway.

With 1.8.0 this doesn't happen any more with nested PloneSandboxes.
Nested DemoStorages aren't a problem, zcml reconfigurations are also not a problem, the Layer will rerigster plone ande dependencies, but:
Persistent tools registered via componentregistry.xml are gone.
The tool is still available in the database, but the registration that has happened in the import step is gone.

Robot login is a proprietary authentication protocol (similar to OAUTH), initially developed for the *OpenAIRE Orphan Repository* to exploit the *OpenAIRE Portal* for authentication, and later adopted by the *INSPIRE* service to exploit *arXiv.org* for authentication.

Today the OpenAIRE Orphan Repository has been replaced by Zenodo, and INSPIRE is little by little upgraded to INSPIRE Labs which sports native authentication (OAUTH-based) from ORCID.

Therefore I'd propose to deprecate it.

INSPIRE will be able to still import data from arXiv using a dedicated module.

I see the fields.Function being pretty useful with in webargs which presently only deals with deserialization. Unfortunately at the moment I have to provide a dummy function for the func argument or I'll hit an exception. It's entirely possible that there's a better field type within Marshmallow for addressing this but I don't think so.

I have a bit of a question mark around whether it makes sense to maintain backward compatibility and leave the signature as is:

```python
class Function(object):
    def __init__(self, func, deserialize=None, **kwargs):
        # func is allowed to be None
        ...
```

or shift to something like

```python
class Function(object):
    def __init__(self, serialize=None, deserialize=None, **kwargs):
        ...
```

which obviously isn't backward compatible but provides a clearer interface. Also, there's a bit of a question mark around whether the class should raise an exception if both serialize and deserialize are missing or whether it should just continue on despite being useless - probably an exception makes sense.

Happy to provide a PR should this change be acceptable.

could this be implemented in SCout?  See publication link
This would complement the severity prediciton when  SIFT and polyphen can not 

http://journals.plos.org/plosone/article?id=10.1371/journal.pone.0077940

Hi, 

Apologies in advance for the lack of an SSCE here, but I'm a bit puzzled by the following error message I got and was hoping some folks on this list could point me in the right direction.

Any idea what could be causing this?

custom_date_field is just a substituted name for a date field I'm actually using.  

```
today = datetime.date.today()
yesterday = today - datetime.timedelta(days = 1)

leads = Lead.objects.filter(custom_date_field__range=(yesterday, today))

for lead in leads:
    print(lead.id)
```
returns:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<console>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/Users/chromakey/.virtualenvs/my_project/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/models/query.py", line 141, in __iter__
    self._fetch_all()
  File "/Users/chromakey/.virtualenvs/my_project/lib/python3.4/site-packages/django/db/models/query.py", line 966, in _fetch_all
    self._result_cache = list(self.iterator())
  File "/Users/chromakey/.virtualenvs/my_project/lib/python3.4/site-packages/salesforce/backend/query.py", line 252, in iterator
    cursor.execute(sql, params)
  File "/Users/chromakey/.virtualenvs/my_project/lib/python3.4/site-packages/salesforce/backend/query.py", line 407, in execute
    response = self.execute_select(q, args)
  File "/Users/chromakey/.virtualenvs/my_project/lib/python3.4/site-packages/salesforce/backend/query.py", line 456, in execute_select
    return handle_api_exceptions(url, self.session.get, _cursor=self)
  File "/Users/chromakey/.virtualenvs/my_project/lib/python3.4/site-packages/salesforce/backend/query.py", line 142, in handle_api_exceptions
    raise base.SalesforceError(data['message'], data, response, verbose)
salesforce.backend.base.SalesforceError: 
Lead.custom_date_field__c BETWEEN 2015-09-01 AND 2015-09-02
                                 ^
ERROR at Row:1:Column:968
unexpected token: 'BETWEEN'
```

```
print(leads.query.get_compiler('salesforce').as_sql())
```
returns:
```
('SELECT <<my_lead_fields>> FROM Lead WHERE Lead.custom_date_field__c BETWEEN %s AND %s', (datetime.date(2015, 9, 1), datetime.date(2015, 9, 2)))
```

A cursory search of the SOQL documentation doesn't seem to turn up anything on using the BETWEEN clause.  From what I can tell, the correct SOQL would be:

```
('SELECT <<my_lead_fields>> FROM Lead WHERE Lead.custom_date_field__c > %s AND Lead.custom_date_field__c < %s', (datetime.date(2015, 9, 1), datetime.date(2015, 9, 2)))
```

You guys do great work on this library (and update it constantly).  If this is indeed the case that the range lookup doesn't work, and I'm not just missing something, I'd like to write a pull request for it.  I'm just not sure where I should stick the custom lookup code, but if you steer me in the right direction I'll take the time to do it.  Thanks!

issue_title: Review the datetime helpers in gcloud._helpers.
issue_body:
`gcloud._helpers` has accumulated a collection of ~10 helper functions related to `datetime` handling. These could perhaps use a review to determine if they are all essential and maximally useful.

The particular situation in which this has come up is a proposal to update `_datetime_to_rfc3339()` to handle time zones as in the following module-private helper function appearing in #1691:

    def _format_timestamp(timestamp):
        """Convert a datetime object to a string as required by the API.

        :type timestamp: :class:`datetime.datetime`
        :param timestamp: A datetime object.

        :rtype: string
        :returns: The formatted timestamp. For example:
            ``"2016-02-17T19:18:01.763000Z"``
        """
        if timestamp.tzinfo is not None:
            # Convert to UTC and remove the time zone info.
            timestamp = timestamp.replace(tzinfo=None) - timestamp.utcoffset()

        return timestamp.isoformat() + 'Z'

issue_title: Inconsistent docstring and code in class Pipeline
issue_body:
#### Description
<!-- Example: Joblib Error thrown when calling fit on LatentDirichletAllocation with evaluate_every > 0-->
The docstrings for multiple methods in the `Pipeline` class state that the methods "Apply transformers/samplers...". However, most of the methods ignore the samplers e.g. the predict method:
https://github.com/scikit-learn-contrib/imbalanced-learn/blob/eafae67994476a0c239bf459d5ea5d875ef9f3f1/imblearn/pipeline.py#L349-L350 vs.
https://github.com/scikit-learn-contrib/imbalanced-learn/blob/eafae67994476a0c239bf459d5ea5d875ef9f3f1/imblearn/pipeline.py#L367-L368

The behaviour appears to be correct (sampling should only be applied when fitting) but the docstrings are misleading.

#### Affected Methods
* predict
* predict_proba
* decision_function
* predict_log_proba
* score

issue_title: Allow masking part of command in logs
issue_body:
Command can contain sensitive data, which shouldn't be written to logs.
Need to implement optional argument (in `**kwargs`), which will receive regex expression to lookup for such string and mask it.

Currently, span objects are converted to an intermediate json format before being sent to an exporter. As a result, there are some stackdriver specific conversions [being applied](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/span.py#L222) to all spans (such as string truncation) that may not necessarily apply to all export formats.

I am proposing to update the [base.Exporter](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/exporters/base.py#L18) emit and export methods to take a raw [span.Span](https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/master/opencensus/trace/span.py#L28) object instead of a dict. Not only will this push stackdriver specific details to the stackdriver exporter, but it would also make the development of new exporters easier since there are less layers of conversion.

Happy to make a PR if everyone is ok with the change.
